title: Most news feeds are designed to keep you scrolling forever.
body:
When we built Headlinne, we had to make a choice about what success actually looks like. Many platforms measure success by time spent on the app. The longer you stay, the more ads they show, and the more 'valuable' the feed becomes to them.

We decided to do the opposite. Our engineering goal was to help you catch up on the news you care about as quickly as possible. We don't want you to live in the app. We want you to read what matters and get on with your day.

To do this, we built our recommendation engine to prioritize relevance over engagement. If you have already read a story from a specific angle, the system knows to move on. It is not trying to trap you in a loop of outrage or endless scrolling.

It is a simple shift in philosophy, but it changes everything about how the feed feels. We believe technology should serve your time, not steal it.
cta: You can see how we are rethinking the news experience at HEADLINNE.com.
